giant cell tumor: lesion located in the metaphysis, exhibiting a predominantly lytic pattern with areas of sclerosis
cortical integrity shows marked thinning but no breach
periosteal reaction present as a smooth lamellar band surrounding the lesion
matrix within the lesion displays a characteristic ground-glass appearance
bone contour demonstrates expansive growth with outward bulging
margin of the lesion has an indistinct border
trabeculae appear destroyed and disorganized within the lesion
soft tissue mass extends beyond the bony confines into adjacent soft tissues
osteochondroma: lesion located in the epiphysis with a mixed lytic and sclerotic pattern, demonstrating thinning of the cortex without breach.
cortical integrity shows expansion with a well-defined sclerotic rim at the margin of the lesion.
periosteal reaction present as a lamellated band surrounding the lesion.
matrix within the lesion exhibits a ground-glass appearance indicative of cartilaginous elements.
bone contour demonstrates outward growth with preservation of the trabecular pattern.
trabeculae appear disorganized with evidence of destruction.
soft tissue mass extends beyond the bony confines into the adjacent soft tissues.
lesion presents with a geographic pattern involving both lytic and sclerotic components.
osteofibroma: lesion located in the metaphysis, presenting as a mixed lytic and sclerotic area with a well-defined sclerotic rim
cortical thinning observed without breach in the diaphyseal region adjacent to the lesion
periosteal reaction noted as a smooth lamellar band surrounding the expansile lytic lesion in the epiphysis
matrix within the lesion exhibits a ground-glass appearance indicative of osteoblastic activity
trabeculae demonstrate disorganized patterns with destruction in the geographic-like distribution of the lesion
soft tissue extension beyond the bony confines forming a small mass in the vicinity of the lytic-sclerotic lesion
margin of the lesion displays an ill-defined border, suggesting potential aggressive behavior
bone contour shows outward expansion consistent with the expansive nature of the lesion
osteosarcoma: lesion located in the metaphysis, exhibiting a mixed lytic and sclerotic pattern with preserved trabecular pattern
cortical thinning observed without evidence of breach in the diaphyseal region
periosteal reaction present as a lamellated band surrounding the expansive lesion in the epiphysis
matrix within the lesion displays a ground-glass appearance indicative of osteosarcoma
bone contour demonstrates expansion with outward growth in the metaphyseal segment
margin of the lesion exhibits an ill-defined border characteristic of osteosarcoma
trabeculae appear destroyed and disorganized within the lytic area of the lesion
soft tissue mass evident extending beyond the bony confines into adjacent soft tissues
simple bone cyst: lesion located in the metaphysis, predominantly lytic with areas of sclerotic borders
cortical thinning observed without breach in the diaphyseal region
periosteal reaction present as a smooth lamellar band surrounding the lesion
matrix within the lesion exhibits a ground-glass appearance
bone contour demonstrates expansive growth with outward margin
trabeculae show signs of destruction and disorganization
intraosseous margins defined by a well-circumscribed sclerotic rim
soft tissue mass not extending beyond the bony confines
synovial osteochondroma: lesion located in the epiphysis with a mixed lytic and sclerotic pattern, showing thinning of the cortex without breach, and a well-defined sclerotic rim at its margin
periosteal reaction present as a lamellated band surrounding a predominantly lytic area with some sclerotic components
matrix within the lesion exhibits a ground-glass appearance, indicative of cartilaginous differentiation
bone contour demonstrates marked expansion with outward growth, while trabeculae appear disorganized and destroyed
soft tissue mass extending beyond the bony confines, forming a distinct extra-skeletal envelope
lesion in the metaphysis characterized by a permeative lytic pattern with preserved underlying trabecular architecture
cortical integrity shows thinning but no breach, with the lesion having an ill-defined margin that blends into adjacent normal bone
trabecular pattern within the lesion is disrupted, with areas of both lytic and sclerotic changes creating a moth-eaten appearance
